A.does: Executes a search by simultaneously tracking all active NFA states (epsilon-closed) at each input position, advancing them in lockstep — guaranteeing linear time with no haystack length limit.

B.does: Executes a search via recursive backtracking over the NFA, but records every (state, input-position) pair in a `Visited` bitset to prevent revisiting, bounding worst-case work at the cost of a maximum haystack length.

comparison: BoundedBacktracker is faster than PikeVM in practice (less overhead per step) but is limited to short haystacks by its visited-capacity budget.
notes: Module-level descriptions confirmed from pikevm.rs:4, backtrack.rs:5, lib.rs:238-245
